

```{python}
# import libraries
import pygame
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# import functions here....
from functions.init import init_grid
from functions.draw import draw
from functions.update import update_sir
from functions.mouse import mouse_to_cell

# States
S = 0
I = 1
R = 2

# Colors
col_S = (50, 100, 255)
col_I = (255, 50, 50)
col_R = (50, 255, 50)
col_grid = (30, 30, 60)

# Params
beta = 0.05   # infection probability
gamma = 0.05  # recovery probability

# Begin Program Body
def main(dimx, dimy, cellsize, S=S, I=I, R=R, beta=beta, gamma=gamma, 
         col_S=col_S, col_I=col_I, col_R=col_R, col_grid=col_grid):
    
    pygame.init()
    surface = pygame.display.set_mode((dimx * cellsize, dimy * cellsize))
    pygame.display.set_caption("Agent-Based SIR Model - Press ESC to finish")
    
    clock = pygame.time.Clock()
    grid = init_grid(dimx, dimy)
    
    running = False
    step = False
    done = False
    
    # epidemic history
    S_hist = []
    I_hist = []
    R_hist = []
    
    print("Instructions:")
    print("- Click cells to infect them")
    print("- Press SPACEBAR to start/pause")
    print("- Press S to step one frame")
    print("- Press C to reset grid")
    print("- Press ESC to finish and see plot")
    print("- Close the pygame window when done")
    
    while not done:
        for event in pygame.event.get():
            if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
                done = True
            
            if event.type == pygame.KEYDOWN:
                if event.key == pygame.K_ESCAPE:
                    done = True
                if event.key == pygame.K_SPACE:
                    running = not running
                    print("Running:" if running else "Paused")
                if event.key == pygame.K_s:
                    step = True
                    print("Step forward")
                if event.key == pygame.K_c:
                    grid = init_grid(dimx, dimy)
                    S_hist = []
                    I_hist = []
                    R_hist = []
                    print("Grid reset")
            
            if event.type == pygame.MOUSEBUTTONDOWN:
                row, col = mouse_to_cell(event.pos, cellsize)
                if event.button == 1:   # left click → infect
                    if grid[row, col] == S:
                        grid[row, col] = I
                        print(f"Infected cell at ({row}, {col})")
                elif event.button == 3: # right click → recover
                    grid[row, col] = R
                    print(f"Recovered cell at ({row}, {col})")
        
        surface.fill(col_grid)
        
        if running or step:
            grid = update_sir(grid, beta, gamma, S=S, I=I, R=R)
            step = False
            total = grid.size
            S_hist.append(np.sum(grid == S) / total)
            I_hist.append(np.sum(grid == I) / total)
            R_hist.append(np.sum(grid == R) / total)
        
        draw(surface, grid, cellsize, S=S, I=I, R=R, 
             col_S=col_S, col_I=col_I, col_R=col_R)
        
        pygame.display.update()
        clock.tick(20)
    
    pygame.quit()
    pygame.display.quit()
    
    # Plot epidemic curves
    if len(S_hist) > 0:
        t = range(len(S_hist))
        plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
        plt.plot(t, S_hist, color=np.array(col_S)/255, label="Susceptible")
        plt.plot(t, I_hist, color=np.array(col_I)/255, label="Infected")
        plt.plot(t, R_hist, color=np.array(col_R)/255, label="Recovered")
        plt.xlabel("Time Step")
        plt.ylabel("Proportion of Population")
        plt.title("Agent-Based SIR Dynamics")
        plt.legend()
        plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.show()
    else:
        print("No simulation data to plot")

# Run main function
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main(100, 70, 8)


```
